app.name: Milestones of Human Progress
app.tagline: The discoveries, inventions and minds that shaped our world

home.title: Milestones of Human Progress
home.subtitle: From the wheat fields of antiquity to CRISPR — explore the discoveries, inventions and institutions that have moved humanity forward, across every era, every domain and every corner of the world.
home.exploreBy: Or pick a way to explore

timeline.title: Timeline
timeline.desc: Travel from antiquity to today through the milestones that changed everything, era by era.

fields.title: By Field
fields.desc: Browse breakthroughs in physics, medicine, computing, space and more.

map.title: World Map
map.desc: See where history was made — and what was discovered near you.

timeline.title: Timeline
timeline.subtitle: Scroll through the great discoveries and inventions, grouped by era — from antiquity to the digital age.

science_tech.title: Science & Technology
science_tech.desc: From Archimedes to CRISPR — the discoveries and inventions that reshaped how we see the world.

economy_industry.title: Economy & Industry
economy_industry.desc: From wheat fields to the assembly line — the trade routes, money and methods that have organized human life.

fieldsIndex.title: By Field
fieldsIndex.subtitle: Domains across two great families of progress. Pick one to follow its breakthroughs through time.

map.title: World Map
map.subtitle: Where each discovery and invention happened.
map.denied: Location unavailable. Showing all sites (centered on Florence).

detail.about: What it was & why it mattered

math.title: Math Search
math.subtitle: Ask a question about equations, theorems or methods from the ingested PDFs. Answers are passages retrieved by semantic search and rendered with LaTeX.
math.searchPlaceholder: e.g. 'derivative of arctan' or 'fundamental theorem of calculus'
math.errorEnv: Math search isn't fully configured — see README § Math RAG.

gallery.title: 3D Math Gallery
gallery.subtitle: Classics from differential geometry — minimal surfaces, knots, chaotic flows, phyllotaxis. Rotate, restyle, watch them morph. Built with Three.js and KaTeX.
gallery.hint: Drag to rotate · scroll to zoom
gallery.askLeoTooltip: Open the chat with a question about this surface
gallery.askLeoPrompt: Tell me about {{name}} — its history, who first studied it, and where it appears in nature or other parts of mathematics.

custom.title: New custom surface
custom.hint: Type three JavaScript expressions for x(u, v), y(u, v), z(u, v) using sin / cos / sqrt / exp / pow / PI etc. u and v come in [0, 2π]. Try a template or ask Leonardo to invent a formula.
custom.name: Name
custom.errNoName: Please give the surface a name.

status.empty: No milestones yet. Run db/schema.sql then db/seed.sql in the Supabase SQL editor.
status.loadError: Couldn't load data. Check your Supabase configuration.

leo.subtitle: Your AI guide
leo.greet: Salve! 🎨 Pleased to meet you — I'm Leonardo. Tell me what catches your eye: a milestone, a formula, a face on the wall. Snap a photo of something curious, or just say it aloud — I'm listening either way.
leo.greetMath: Salve! 🎨 I love when someone wants to look closely at the maths. Type a formula, snap a page from a book, or just tell me what you're trying to understand — let's work it out together.
leo.greetGallery: Beautiful, aren't they? 🎨 Each of these surfaces hides an old secret of geometry. Pick one and ask me anything — why it bends like that, who first wrote it down, where it shows up in nature.
leo.greetMilestone: Sitting with one of the great moments, are we? 🎨 Ask me anything about it — what came before, who carried the idea forward, why it still matters today.
leo.cameraUnsupported: Camera not available in this browser.

admin.confirmDelete: Delete this row? This cannot be undone.

login.title: Admin sign-in
login.subtitle: Sign in to edit milestones, figures, locations and the RAG library.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Current row counts — click a card to manage that table.

rag.heading: RAG library
rag.title: Title
rag.confirmDelete: Delete {{filename}} and all of its chunks?

upload.title: Add new PDFs
upload.subtitle: Drop in one or several PDFs — we extract markdown page-by-page using Claude vision (math comes out as LaTeX), then chunk and embed via Voyage. The CLI ingest is still available for bulk runs.

ai.label: AI
ai.translateOthers: Translate this into the other two languages

surfaces.heading: 3D Gallery surfaces
surfaces.name: Display name
surfaces.hint: u and v come in [0, 2π]. Allowed: sin, cos, sinh, cosh, sqrt, exp, log, pow, abs, PI, E, TAU. Save will refuse expressions that don't compile.
surfaces.publishTip: Make this surface visible on the public /gallery
surfaces.unpublishTip: Hide this surface from the public /gallery
surfaces.saveFirst: Save the surface first, then publish.
surfaces.codeLabel: Source — assign x, y, z (u, v ∈ [0, 2π])
surfaces.codeHint: Multi-line JS body — declare locals, use sin/cos/etc., assign x, y, z. Whitelisted scope; disallowed: window, document, fetch, import, eval, globalThis, self.
surfaces.slugHint: Lowercase id used to dedupe against built-ins (e.g. 'klein'). Leave blank for user-created surfaces.
surfaces.paramsSchemaHint: Array of { key, label, min, max, step, default, precision? } — controls the sliders visitors see under the viewport.
surfaces.metadataHint: Object with extras per kind: parametric/morph → { uvSegments }, attractor → { dt, x0, y0, z0 }, points → { pointSize, animated }.
surfaces.codeLabelMorph: Source — assign x, y, z; use u, v, time, p
surfaces.codeLabelAttractor: Source — assign dx, dy, dz from x, y, z, p
surfaces.codeLabelPoints: Source — assign x, y, z from i, n, p
surfaces.codeHintMorph: Bodies receive (u, v, time, p). Same scope as parametric — sin/cos/sinh/cosh/PI etc. Use `time` to drive animation.
surfaces.codeHintAttractor: Body returns the derivative (dx, dy, dz) from current state (x, y, z) and params p. The wrapper integrates with fixed-step Euler.
surfaces.codeHintPoints: Body places point i of n at (x, y, z). Wrapper iterates and packs into a buffer.
surfaces.builtinHint: Built-in geometries use a Three.js helper (TorusKnotGeometry, etc.). Set the params via the JSON below — no source code needed.
surfaces.saveDefaults: Save as defaults for this surface
surfaces.saveDefaultsTip: Persist the current preview choices so /gallery visitors see this surface with them
surfaces.viewSaved: Defaults saved — public /gallery will use them on next load.
